title: Unable to copy or capture whole chat session content.
body_excerpt: Type: Bug The following report was generated by Visual Studio Code Ask Agent. Here is a concise internal engineering report you can send: Subject: Internal Engineering Report — Inability to Capture Full Chat Session Content and Transcript-Generation Loop Summary: During a recent Copilot chat session, the user reported inability to capture the full chat session content through the available chat UI controls. Attempts to use “Copy all”, “Copy conversation”, and “Export chat” were unsuccessful or ineffective. In addition, when asked to generate a clean transcript of the entire session, the assistant entered a repetitive response loop and required a stop command to terminate the behavior. Observed Behavior: The chat UI did not provide a reliable mechanism to copy or export the full session content. Ctrl+A did not select the complete chat history as expected. Copying only captured a partial segment of the conversation rather than the whole session. Transcript generation repeat

title: BUG: DataFrame.loc partial assignment upcasts dtype of untouched columns
body_excerpt:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

``` python 

import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"A": [1, 2, 3], "B": [4, 5, 6], "C": [7, 8, 9]})
print("before:")
print(df.dtypes)

df.loc[df["A"] > 1, "B"] = pd.Series([99], index=[2])
print("\nafter:")
print(df.dtypes)
```

```
Output:

before:
A    int64
B    int64
C    int64
dtype: object

after:
A    float64
B    float64
C    float64
dtype: object
```

### Issue Description

Assigning a `Series` to a subset of rows of one column via `df.loc[mask, "col"] = series` silently upcasts the `dtype` of other, untouched columns in a single-dtype DataFrame (and

title: BUG: Assignment on negative slice gives error
body_excerpt:
### Pandas version checks

- [x] I have checked that this issue has not already been reported.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [latest version](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/whatsnew/index.html) of pandas.

- [x] I have confirmed this bug exists on the [main branch](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/dev/getting_started/install.html#installing-the-development-version-of-pandas) of pandas.


### Reproducible Example

```python
import pandas as pd
s=pd.Series([2, 13, 7, 9, 3], index=['a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e'])

s.iloc[::-2]= [11, 5, 15]
print(s)
```

### Issue Description

The negative slice has 3 elements and should be assigned three values but it gives 
ValueError: cannot set using a slice indexer with a different length than the value

Either the error message should be changed that "this feature is currently not supported" as the current error message is clearly wrong or this feature should be included as it is intutively correct

### Expected Behavior

a    15
b    13
c
